us_equities.prose: Stocks rebounded broadly after the United States and Iran signed an interim peace deal, with small-cap and broad indices leading the advance in a risk-on tone.

cited_sources.title: U.S. Stocks Poised to Close Week on Strong Note

rates_and_dollar.prose: Treasury yields and the dollar rose after the Federal Reserve held rates in a unanimous decision at Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chairman, with markets pricing in a higher-for-longer rate path.

cited_sources.title: Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise on First Fed Move Under Warsh

commodities.prose: Oil fell over the week as the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and progress on a US-Iran peace deal raised expectations of a significant surge in supply, with the IEA warning of a major oil overhang ahead.

cited_sources.title: OPEC chief dismisses IEA supply glut forecast as 'critical' Strait of Hormuz reopens

stock:SPCX.prose: SpaceX shares pulled back for a second consecutive day as initial post-IPO investor enthusiasm showed signs of waning following the stock's sharp run-up after its historic debut.

cited_sources.title: SpaceX stock sinks for a second day as company slips below Amazon in market cap
